project.title: ATTS: Agentic Test-Time Scaling via Adaptive Exploration
project.description: A delegated sequential test-time scaling framework where a lightweight orchestrator adaptively controls a pool of candidate solutions through a single action (explore), deciding when to stop and synthesizing the final answer from accumulated evidence.
project.field: Machine Learning / LLM Reasoning / Test-Time Scaling

research.coreQuestion: How to adaptively allocate test-time compute over a semantic pool of candidate hypotheses without explicit search trees or handcrafted stopping thresholds?
research.approach: A lightweight orchestrator repeatedly decides whether to call explore (dispatch a fresh independent solver) or stop and synthesize. The orchestrator never solves the problem itself -- it manages evidence. The control logic is benchmark-agnostic: only the explorer prompt and answer schema change across tasks.
keyContributions: Delegated sequential test-time scaling with a single-action interface (explore)
Adaptive compute allocation via semantic evidence management rather than heuristic rules
Benchmark-agnostic controller transferable across text-only and multimodal tasks
Evaluation on 6 benchmarks spanning math, science, code, and multimodal reasoning
methods: Claude Agent SDK with MCP tools

datasets.evaluation: HLE-Verified Gold (100 samples), LiveCodeBench v6, BabyVision, GPQA-Diamond, AIME 2025/2026

currentStatus.notes: Paper draft is structurally complete (Introduction, Related Work, Methodology, Experiments with main results + ablations + analysis, Limitations, Conclusion, Appendices, NeurIPS Checklist). Currently iterating on revisions.
